concerns.text: FR-002 mandates exclusion of participants with mean FD > 0.3 mm. This directly contradicts Constitution Principle VII (Motion Artifact Exclusion), which mandates exclusion only for FD > 3 mm. The spec fails to resolve this conflict or document a formal amendment, creating a fatal internal inconsistency between the project's functional requirements and its governing constitution.

FR-005 requires a regression model predicting 'baseline working-memory performance' while controlling for 'baseline cognitive score'. The spec does not define 'baseline cognitive score' as distinct from the outcome variable. If they are the same, this creates circular reasoning/perfect collinearity; if different, the term is undefined. This makes the requirement logically incoherent and untestable.

FR-008 requires the pipeline to complete on a GitHub Actions free-tier runner (2 cores, 7GB RAM) within 24 hours for N=85 participants. This contradicts the computational reality of running fMRIPrep 23.1.3 (mandated by FR-002 and Constitution Principle VI) on 85 subjects with those resource constraints. The requirement is mutually incompatible with the technical constraints defined in the same section and the constitution.

SC-001 requires a statistically significant result (p < 0.05) for the frontoparietal coefficient. This is a scientific outcome, not a system capability. A system cannot 'MUST' produce a specific statistical result; it can only produce the analysis. This conflates a success metric with a functional requirement, creating a logical category error in the spec.

FR-009 requires a 'fatal error' if participant IDs are missing, but the Edge Cases section states the system must 'skip that participant and log a warning' for missing data. While 'missing scan' and 'missing ID' are slightly different, the terminology and handling logic are inconsistent between the FR and Edge Cases, creating ambiguity in the error handling protocol.
